name: Little Fried Pies
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: Southern Living
Preparation Time: 0:20
1. Combine the dried apples and water in a large saucepan. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer covered for about 30 minutes or until tender. Cool slightly, then mash if necessary.
2. Stir in the sugar and cinnamon. Set aside.
3. Divide the fried pie pastry into thirds. Roll each portion to a thickness of 1/4 inch on waxed paper, then cut into 5-inch circles.
4. Place about 2 tablespoons of apple mixture on half of each pastry circle.
5. To seal the pies, dip your fingers in water and moisten the edges of the pastry circles. Fold the circles over so that they are even, pressing firmly to seal with a fork dipped in flour.
6. Heat vegetable oil to 375 degrees Fahrenheit in a large skillet.
7. Fry the pies until golden brown on both sides, turning once. Drain well on paper towels.
8. Sprinkle with sugar while warm.
Fried Pie Pastry:
3 cups All-purpose flour
1 tsp Salt
3/4 cup Shortening
1 Egg; beaten
1/4 cup Water
1 tsp Vinegar
Combine the flour and salt. Cut in the shortening until the mixture resembles coarse meal.
Add the egg and water, then sprinkle with vinegar. Mix lightly with a fork until dry ingredients are moistened.
Shape into a ball, wrap in waxed paper, and chill at least 1 hour. This yields pastry for about 1 dozen 5-inch pies.
